title: Oats Chilla (Savoury Oat Pancakes)

subs.name: ragi flour (finger millet) for a calcium-rich variation
moong dal flour or extra oat flour — keeps it gluten-free
grated zucchini, beetroot or spinach for colour and micronutrients

steps.text: If you have a mixie or blender, pulse the rolled oats to a coarse powder — about 8-10 pulses, not a fine flour. This gives the chilla body without making it gummy. Alternatively use quick-cooking oats as-is, which soften faster in the batter.

Combine oat powder, besan, grated onion, grated carrot, green chilli, cumin seeds, turmeric, coriander leaves and salt in a mixing bowl. Gradually whisk in water until you get a smooth, pourable batter slightly thicker than dosa batter. Rest for 5 minutes so the oats can absorb the moisture.
Heat a tawa on medium-high until a few drops of water skitter and evaporate on contact. Smear very lightly with a few drops of oil using a kitchen paper or the back of a ladle.

Pour a ladleful of batter onto the centre of the tawa and spread quickly in concentric circles to a thin 15-17 cm round. Drizzle a few drops of oil around the edges. Cook for 2-3 minutes until the surface turns matte and the edges look set and slightly crisp.

Flip carefully with a wide spatula and cook the other side 1-2 minutes until light golden spots appear. Transfer to a plate and repeat with remaining batter. Serve hot with green chutney or low-fat curd on the side.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: oats → ragi flour (finger millet) for a calcium-rich variation; besan → moong dal flour or extra oat flour — keeps it gluten-free; carrot → grated zucchini, beetroot or spinach for colour and micronutrients
